github.copilot.chat.commitMessageGenerat.text:
Format: <type>(<scope>): <subject> <emoji>

Allowed types: feat, fix, docs, refactor, test, chore, perf, ci, build, style, revert.
Allowed scopes: asset, attachment, checkup, configuration, contribution, document, feature-resolver, fom, gmaor-report, metrics, operation, release, script, shared, task, time-entry, work-order, migrator, deps.
Omit scope only for cross-cutting changes that don't fit a single scope.

Header: max 100 chars total. Use imperative mood (e.g. 'add', 'fix', 'remove', not 'added', 'fixes', 'removed'). Be specific: prefer 'add search endpoint for attachments' over 'update attachment code'.
Body: wrap at 72 chars per line. Explain WHAT changed and WHY, not HOW (the diff shows how). Skip the body for trivial changes (typos, dependency bumps, simple renames).
End the header with a single relevant emoji: ✨ feat, 🐛 fix, 📚 docs, 🔧 refactor, ✅ test, 🔨 chore, ⚡ perf, 🔒 security, 🗑️ deprecation/removal.
Footer: add 'Refs: <branch-name>' using the current Git branch name. For breaking changes, add a 'BREAKING CHANGE: <description>' line.

github.copilot.chat.reviewSelection.inst.text: Provide a clear and concise summary of the changes made in the code. This can help other developers understand the context and purpose of the changes
Highlight any potential issues or areas of concern in the code. This can help other developers identify and address any problems before they become bigger issues
Offer suggestions for improvement or alternative approaches to the code. This can help other developers learn and grow their skills while also improving the overall quality of the codebase
